questions.stem: What is GitHub Copilot primarily designed for?

choices.A: It's designed as a tool for managing GitHub repositories.
choices.B: It's an AI-powered coding assistant to augment a developer's capabilities.
choices.C: It's software for automatic code testing and deployment.
choices.D: It's a platform for collaborative coding and project management. GitHub Copilot is a sophisticated AI-powered coding assistant, built on top of a large language model. Its primary goal is to augment a developer's capabilities, making coding more efficient and is thus designed to streamline the coding process by suggesting lines or blocks of code.

questions.stem: Which of the following is a limitation of GitHub Copilot? Select two choices.

choices.A: It cannot integrate with any Integrated Development Environments (IDEs).
choices.B: It only functions with a few specific programming languages.
choices.C: Suggestions may sometimes be outdated or include security vulnerabilities.
choices.D: It requires an internet connection to function. While GitHub Copilot is adept at generating code and assisting with documentation, its suggestions reflect its training data. This means that the suggestions may occasionally be outdated or include security vulnerabilities, making it crucial for developers to review and ensure the final code is robust and secure. GitHub Copilot connects to GitHub's server directly from your environment, via a secure HTTPS connection. This means that it needs an internet connection.

questions.stem: Which of the following is a key feature of GitHub Copilot's recent updates?

choices.A: Copilot has started supporting all programming languages uniformly.
choices.B: Copilot can now use related files for context, not just the current file.
choices.C: Copilot now offers real-time collaboration features.
choices.D: Copilot now provides automatic debugging for any code it generates. Recent updates hint at Copilot using related files for context, rather than focusing solely on the current file. The exact behavior remains somewhat unpredictable and often difficult to observe. This development is seen as an improvement in its context awareness, although the exact behavior is described as somewhat unpredictable and often difficult to observe.

choices.C: To automatically generate code without developer input.
choices.D: To act as placeholders for future code sections. Docstrings are used to provide context and guide Copilot in generating appropriate code. Docstrings are multi- line strings that serve as documentation as well as to give Copilot specific instructions or context for the code it needs to generate.

This means that the suggestions may occasionally be outdated or include security vulnerabilities, making it crucial for developers to review and ensure the final code is robust and secure. GitHub Copilot connects to GitHub's server directly from your environment, via a secure HTTPS connection. This means that it needs an internet connection.

questions.stem: What are the potential limitations of GitHub Copilot in maintaining existing codebases?

choices.A: GitHub Copilot’s suggestions are always aware of the entire codebase.
choices.B: GitHub Copilot can refactor and optimize the entire codebase up to 10,000 lines of code.
choices.C: GitHub Copilot can independently manage and resolve all merge conflicts in version control.
choices.D: GitHub Copilot might not fully understand the context and dependencies within a large codebase.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of the duplication detection filter in GitHub Copilot?

choices.A: To compare user-generated code against a private repository for potential matches.
choices.B: To encourage the user to follow coding best practices preventing code duplication.
choices.C: To allow administrators to control which suggestions are visible to developers based on custom criteria.
choices.D: To detect and block suggestions that match public code snippets on GitHub if they contain about 150 characters.

questions.stem: How does GitHub Copilot assist developers in reducing the amount of manual boilerplate code they write?

choices.A: By refactoring the entire codebase to eliminate boilerplate code without developer input.
choices.B: By suggesting code snippets that can be reused across different parts of the project.
choices.C: By engaging in real-time collaboration with multiple developers to write boilerplate code.
choices.D: By predicting future coding requirements and pre-emptively generating boilerplate code

questions.stem: What are the different ways to give context to GitHub Copilot to get more precise responses? Each correct answer presents part of the solution. (Choose two.)

choices.A: Engage with chat participants such as @workspace to incorporate collaborative context into the responses.
choices.B: Access developer’s previous projects and code repositories to understand their coding style without explicit permission.
choices.C: Utilize to interpret developer’s thoughts and intentions without any code or comments.
choices.D: Utilize chat variables like #file and #editors to anchor the conversation within the specific context of the files or editors in use.

questions.stem: If you are working on open source projects, GitHub Copilot Individual can be paid:

questions.stem: What do you check when GitHub Copilot content exclusions are not working? Each correct answer presents part of the solution. (Choose two.)

choices.A: When the user prompts with @workspace.
choices.B: When the repository level settings allow overrides by the user.

choices.D: If the contents of an excluded file are referenced in code that is not excluded, for example function calls.

questions.stem: When crafting prompts for GitHub Copilot, what is a recommended strategy to enhance the relevance of the generated code?
